votes.reason: The Python code faithfully implements the custom string parsing logic (handling signs, dot/comma separators, and integer/fractional parts) and comparison logic defined in the Coq specification. It uses `fractions.Fraction` to perform exact arithmetic, matching the behavior of Coq's real numbers on the parsed values, and correctly handles the `VFloat` case by converting binary floats to their exact rational representation.
The Python code faithfully translates the Coq definitions, using fractions to exactly represent real numbers and accurately mirroring the string parsing logic.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq specification, including the string parsing logic, exact rational arithmetic using Fraction, and the comparison logic.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. Both use the same parsing logic for strings (handling '.' and ',' as decimal separators, optional sign, converting to exact rational/real values). VInt maps to exact integer values, VFloat maps to the exact real value (Python's Fraction(float) gives the exact rational representation of the IEEE 754 float, matching Coq's treatment of R), and VStr uses identical parsing. The comparison logic (return larger value, None if equal) matches exactly. All helper functions (is_digit, digit_val, digits_to_nat, split_on_sep, parse_string, string_to_R) implement the same algorithms as their Coq counterparts.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. Both use the same parsing logic for strings (handling '.' and ',' as decimal separators, optional sign, integer and fractional parts). The Python uses Fraction for exact arithmetic which matches Coq's real number semantics. VInt converts to exact rational, VFloat converts the float to its exact rational representation (matching Coq's IZR/real semantics for the stored value), and VStr parses identically. The comparison logic (return b if a<b, return a if b<a, return None if equal) matches exactly. All helper functions (is_digit, digit_val, digits_to_nat, split_on_sep, parse_string) implement the same algorithms as their Coq counterparts.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. Both use the same parsing logic for strings (handling '.' and ',' as decimal separators, optional sign, converting to exact numeric values), the same comparison logic (return larger value or None if equal), and the same value extraction for VInt, VFloat, and VStr types. The Python uses Fraction for exact arithmetic which matches Coq's real number semantics for concrete comparisons. The helper functions (is_digit, digit_val, digits_to_nat, split_on_sep, parse_string, string_to_R) all implement the same algorithms as their Coq counterparts.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: it parses strings with optional sign and '.'/',' decimal separator, rejects invalid formats, converts to exact rationals, handles ints/floats consistently, and compares values returning the original operand or None on equality or parse failure.

String parsing (sign, '.' or ',', at least one integer digit), numeric conversion, and comparison logic match Coq: parse to number or None, map VInt/VFloat/VStr to numbers, then return the larger input or None if equal. Python uses Fraction for exact comparisons, preserving concrete outcomes.

Python mirrors Coq logic for parsing strings (sign, digits, '.' or ','), exact value construction (ints as rationals, floats as exact float rationals), and comparison/return behavior. It returns the same option: larger input, the other input, or None if equal or parse fails.
